sourceMovie.title: Antz
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Antz for the same reason it broke through in 1998: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a comedy rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Eric Darnell keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The animation and comedy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Moana
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Antz clicked for you, Moana (2016) is a strong next stop: both trade in animation and comedy, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Antz?
faq.answer: Try Moana, Minions, A Bug's Life—each shares DNA with Antz while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Antz?
faq.answer: Antz (1998) is best described as Animation, Comedy, Adventure. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Antz worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.2/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this animation lane.
